description: Scoring-based career matching with gender filtering. Frontend filters careers by tags, adapts question count (10-15), shows results when pool is small enough. NLP summary fallback when pool is still large.

careers.label: Video Game & Software Retail Specialist — works with product knowledge of software and video games, customer service, and sales techniques

questions.question: Let's start! What's your current education level?

options.label: I haven't started university yet
I'm in university or have a Bachelor's degree
I have a Master's degree or higher

questions.question: What is your gender?

options.label: Male
Female

questions.question: What kind of work excites you the most?

options.label: Helping and caring for people — in hospitals, clinics, or communities
Teaching and training others
Leading teams and managing projects
Building physical things — structures, machines, systems
Creating technology — writing code, building apps, or working with AI
Solving business, financial, or legal problems
Fixing, repairing, and maintaining things with my hands
Operating vehicles — flying planes, piloting drones, or navigating ships

questions.question: What kind of healthcare work interests you most?

all_options.label: Nursing and midwifery — caring for patients directly
Lab work — running medical tests and diagnostics
Emergency care — responding to crises and saving lives
Specialist medical — dentistry, optometry, or specific body systems
Working with refugees, elderly, or justice system
Veterinary care — treating and helping animals
Assisting vets and caring for animals

questions.question: Where would you feel most at home in healthcare?

options.label: On the ward — moving between patients, checking vitals, and doing procedures
In a lab or office — analyzing test results, reading scans, writing reports
A mix — some patient contact and some behind-the-scenes work

questions.question: What kind of healthcare rhythm suits you best?

options.label: Independently — a solo practitioner, researcher, or specialist
In a team — nurses, doctors, and specialists working together
Both — sometimes seeing patients alone, sometimes in a care team

questions.question: Would you prefer direct patient care or diagnostics and research?

options.label: Direct patient care — talking to patients, treating symptoms, comforting families
Diagnostics and research — lab analysis, imaging, pharmaceutical development
A bit of both — clinical work with some research on the side

questions.question: What trait describes you best in a medical setting?

options.label: Precision — I thrive on accurate diagnoses and detailed science
Creativity — I like finding new treatment approaches and innovations
Empathy — patient comfort and emotional support is what drives me
Leadership — I want to manage a department or lead a medical team

questions.question: What kind of technology work excites you?
What does your ideal day in tech look like?

options.label: Wiring up hardware, configuring servers, or tinkering with devices
Writing code, designing systems, or analyzing data on a screen
A mix — some coding and some physical setup or testing

questions.question: How do you prefer to work on tech projects?

options.label: Deep solo work — headphones on, shipping features and fixing bugs
In a squad — standups, pair programming, code reviews together
Flexible — independent coding with regular team check-ins

questions.question: Would you rather build the product or help users?

options.label: Help users — tech support, UX research, or client-facing development
Build the product — backend systems, algorithms, infrastructure
Both — full-stack work touching users and systems

questions.question: What energizes you most in a tech role?

options.label: Optimizing — performance tuning, debugging, and making things efficient
Creating — designing interfaces, building new features from scratch
Mentoring — code reviews, teaching junior devs, writing docs
Architecting — making high-level decisions, owning system design

questions.question: What would you like to build or design?
Where do you see yourself working as an engineer?

options.label: On-site — construction sites, factories, or field inspections
In an office — CAD designs, simulations, and structural analysis
Both — designing in the office and testing in the field

questions.question: How do you prefer to approach engineering projects?

options.label: Solo — deep focus on my own calculations, designs, and research
Collaborative — working closely with other engineers and contractors
Mixed — independent analysis with team coordination on larger projects

questions.question: Would you prefer designing for clients or optimizing technical systems?

options.label: Client-facing — presenting designs, meeting stakeholders, managing expectations
System-focused — stress testing, material analysis, technical optimization
Both — understanding client needs and translating them into technical solutions

questions.question: What excites you most in engineering?

options.label: Precision — exact measurements, quality control, safety standards
Innovation — prototyping new designs and pushing boundaries
Collaboration — mentoring junior engineers and training field workers
Management — leading project teams and overseeing schedules and budgets

questions.question: What would you like to fix or work on?

all_options.label: Electrical work in railways or mines
Electrical work on ships and boats
Plumbing — installing and fixing water pipes
HVAC — heating, ventilation, and air conditioning

questions.question: What type of hands-on work appeals to you most?

options.label: Heavy physical work — welding, lifting, climbing, working outdoors
Precision work — wiring circuits, reading blueprints, troubleshooting
A combination — some heavy work and some detailed technical tasks

questions.question: How do you prefer to handle trade jobs?

options.label: Solo jobs — visiting homes or small sites on my own
Crew-based — working with a team on large construction or industrial projects
Both — some solo callouts and some team projects

questions.question: Do you prefer dealing with clients or focusing on the technical work?

options.label: Client-facing — quoting jobs, explaining work, building customer relationships
Technical focus — just doing the work, minimal client interaction
Both — meeting clients then getting hands dirty with the installation

questions.question: What kind of tradesperson are you?

options.label: By-the-book — follow codes, safety standards, and regulations precisely
Problem-solver — improvise solutions when things don't go as planned
Trainer — enjoy teaching apprentices and sharing skills
Foreman — want to lead crews and manage job sites

questions.question: What would you like to operate or fly?
Where do you see yourself in aviation?

options.label: In the cockpit — hands on the controls, flying the aircraft
In operations — flight planning, navigation charts, weather analysis
Both — flying and handling operational duties

questions.question: What kind of flying career structure appeals to you?

options.label: Solo missions — single-pilot operations or independent flight instruction
Crew-based — multi-crew cockpit with co-pilots and cabin crew
Flexible — sometimes solo, sometimes with a crew

questions.question: Would you prefer passenger-facing flying or mission-focused operations?

options.label: Passenger-facing — airlines, charters, flight instruction
Mission-focused — cargo, surveillance, search and rescue, drones
Both — a mix of passenger and mission flying

questions.question: What draws you most to aviation?

options.label: Precision — checklists, instruments, and technical mastery
Adventure — exploring new routes, challenging weather, remote destinations
Teaching — sharing flying knowledge as an instructor
Command — being the captain, making critical decisions

questions.question: Who would you like to teach?
What kind of teaching environment do you prefer?

options.label: Active — hands-on activities, experiments, field trips with students
Structured — lectures, lesson planning, and academic assessments
Both — classroom teaching with interactive, practical elements

questions.question: How do you prefer to manage your teaching?

options.label: Independently — running my own classroom, tutoring, or private lessons
In a team — co-teaching, department meetings, collaborative curriculum
A blend — teaching my own class but collaborating with colleagues

questions.question: Would you prefer classroom teaching or curriculum design and administration?

options.label: Classroom — face-to-face with students, lecturing, and mentoring
Behind the scenes — curriculum design, assessment writing, e-learning
Both — teaching and also shaping the programme

questions.question: What kind of educator do you aspire to be?

options.label: Methodical — structured lessons, clear rubrics, data-driven progress tracking
Innovative — project-based learning, creative exercises, new teaching methods
Nurturing — prioritizing student well-being, emotional support, and inclusion
Administrative — heading a department, driving school strategy, leading staff

questions.question: What kind of problems would you like to solve?

all_options.label: Tracking money and checking financial records
Studying how economies and markets work

questions.question: What kind of financial work environment do you prefer?

options.label: Out in the field — auditing businesses, visiting clients' offices
At a desk — spreadsheets, financial models, and reports
Both — some fieldwork and some desk analysis

questions.question: How do you prefer to handle your financial workload?

options.label: Solo — independent analysis, personal bookkeeping clients
Collaborative — working in a finance team, joint audits, group strategy
Mixed — independent research with team decision-making

questions.question: Would you prefer working with numbers or advising clients?

options.label: Advising — meeting clients, explaining finances, giving investment advice
Number-crunching — building models, analyzing trends, forecasting
Both — analyzing data and presenting findings to clients

questions.question: What kind of finance professional do you want to be?

options.label: Detail-oriented — auditing, compliance, making sure every figure is correct
Strategic — finding investment opportunities, building creative portfolios
Advisor — guiding people's financial futures, wealth management
Executive — CFO track, managing entire finance departments

questions.question: What kind of team or project would you want to lead?

all_options.label: Technology projects — software, apps, IT systems

questions.question: What does your ideal business work day look like?

options.label: On the floor — warehouse visits, supply chain checks, hands-on operations
At a desk — strategy docs, market analysis, presentations
Both — boardroom strategy and on-the-ground execution

questions.question: How do you see yourself contributing in a business?

options.label: As a solo consultant — advising companies independently
In a team — cross-functional projects, department collaboration
Flexible — independent analysis with regular team presentations

questions.question: Would you prefer strategy and analytics or people management?

options.label: People management — recruiting, training, performance reviews, HR
Strategy and analytics — market research, data analysis, operations planning
Both — managing people while using data to drive decisions

questions.question: What kind of business role suits your personality?

options.label: Analyst — diving deep into data, metrics, and process optimization
Innovator — launching new products, branding, and marketing campaigns
People person — HR, talent development, organizational culture
Executive — CEO track, making high-stakes decisions, leading organizations

questions.question: What kind of legal work environment appeals to you?

options.label: In court — standing before judges, cross-examining witnesses
At a desk — researching case law, drafting contracts, writing briefs
Both — preparing cases at my desk and arguing them in court

questions.question: How do you prefer to handle legal casework?

options.label: Solo practice — running my own cases from start to finish
Firm-based — paralegals, associates, and partners collaborating on cases
Flexible — independent research with team strategy meetings

questions.question: Would you prefer client-facing advocacy or behind-the-scenes legal research?

options.label: Client advocacy — meeting clients, court appearances, negotiation
Legal research — case law analysis, regulatory compliance, document review
Both — researching cases and presenting them to clients and courts

questions.question: What kind of legal professional do you aspire to be?

options.label: Methodical — precise legal analysis, statutory interpretation, contract review
Persuasive — creative argumentation, courtroom drama, clever negotiations
Advocate — fighting for clients' rights, pro bono work, social justice
Partner — leading a firm, managing associates, shaping legal strategy

questions.question: What's your preferred architecture work setting?

options.label: On-site — visiting construction sites, supervising builds, checking progress
In the studio — 3D modeling, rendering, and design development
Both — designing in the studio and reviewing on-site

questions.question: How do you prefer to work on design projects?

options.label: Independently — developing my own design vision and concepts
In a firm — collaborating with other architects, engineers, and contractors
Both — my own creative direction with team input and collaboration

questions.question: Would you prefer designing for people or solving structural challenges?

options.label: For people — client meetings, understanding needs, community spaces
Structural — technical drawings, building codes, material specifications
Both — balancing client vision with technical feasibility

questions.question: What kind of architect do you want to be?

options.label: Technical — structural engineering details, building regulations, load analysis
Artistic — bold aesthetics, innovative forms, award-winning designs
Community-focused — accessible design, public spaces, user experience
Principal — running my own firm, managing projects, winning contracts

questions.question: Which of these broad areas sounds most interesting to you?

all_options.label: Helping people stay healthy (hospitals, clinics, therapy)
Building and designing things (machines, buildings, systems)
Flying planes, drones, or working in aviation
Working with money, numbers, and financial decisions
Running businesses, managing teams, or organizing projects
